mnemonics.title: ANCOVA Assumptions
mnemonics.content: NHLIL: Normality, Homogeneity of variances, Linearity, Independence, Homogeneity of regression slopes

mnemonics.title: ANCOVA Purpose
mnemonics.content: REPT: Reduce error variance, Increase power, Precision, Control for confounders

mnemonics.title: F-test in ANCOVA
mnemonics.content: F = MSB_adjusted / MSW_adjusted - comparing adjusted group means

acronyms.usage: Statistical technique combining ANOVA and regression
SS divided by degrees of freedom

visualTips.visualization: Imagine all groups lined up at the same starting point (mean covariate value) - the adjusted means show where they would land if they started equally
Think of parallel railway tracks - the covariate-dependent relationship should have the same slope (angle) across all treatment groups
Imagine a funnel - the covariate 'funnels' away some of the error variance, making the remaining differences clearer
Picture a pie chart where Total SS is divided into slices - one slice is treatment (SSB), another is error (SSW), and the covariate takes another slice

analogies.analogy: ANOVA is like comparing heights of students from different classes without knowing their ages. ANCOVA is like comparing heights after accounting for age - giving a fairer comparison.
A covariate is like a life jacket in water - it supports (accounts for) some of what keeps you afloat (variance), making it easier to see if the life ring (treatment) is really helping.
Adjusted means are like test scores normalized to the same difficulty level - if everyone took the test under identical conditions.
Including a covariate is like using a stabilizer in photography - it removes the 'shake' (unwanted variation), giving a clearer picture of the true differences.
